title: 2.0 Plants
description: Quiz covering Unit 2 of Grade 10 Biology: plant characteristics, structure, reproduction, photosynthesis, transport, response, medicinal plants, and Ethiopian botanists.

questions.question: What are plants described as due to their ability to produce their own food?
What is a distinctive feature of plant cells that is not found in animal cells?
options: A rigid cell wall made of cellulose
questions.correctAnswer: A rigid cell wall made of cellulose

questions.question: Which plant group produces naked seeds that are not covered by a fruit?
What is the reproductive structure of gymnosperms?
What is the broadest part of a leaf that provides a large surface area for collecting light?
Which type of root system is a feature of dicot plants?
Which layer of the leaf contains the largest number of chloroplasts and carries out most of the photosynthesis?
Which layer of the stem is composed of collenchyma cells that provide mechanical strength?
What is the function of the endodermis in the root?
options: To help move water and nutrients from the cortex into the xylem
To give rise to lateral roots
To absorb water from the soil
questions.correctAnswer: To help move water and nutrients from the cortex into the xylem

questions.question: In gymnosperms, which structure contains the egg cell?
What is the female reproductive organ of a flower called?
The transfer of pollen grains from an anther to a stigma on the same plant is called:
What develops directly from the zygote after fertilization in flowering plants?
What are the three main parts of a seed?
options: Seed embryo, cotyledon or endosperm, and seed coat
questions.correctAnswer: Seed embryo, cotyledon or endosperm, and seed coat

questions.question: How many cotyledons does a monocot seed have?
Seeds that have hairy or winged structures are most likely dispersed by:
What is the role of water in seed germination?
options: It supplies carbon for the growing seedling
It hydrates the seed coat and enables enzymatic hydrolysis
It provides energy for the growing embryo
It cools the seed during germination
questions.correctAnswer: It hydrates the seed coat and enables enzymatic hydrolysis

questions.question: According to the World Health Organization, approximately how many people in developing countries rely on plant medicines for their health care?
Which Ethiopian botanist was elected as a Foreign Member of the Royal Society in London in 2018?
What are the two distinct parts of a chloroplast?
What colour of light is mainly reflected by chlorophyll, causing leaves to appear green?
What gas is released into the atmosphere as a result of photolysis during the light reaction?
What is the immediate carbohydrate product of the dark reaction in photosynthesis?
What colour change indicates the presence of starch when iodine solution is applied to a leaf?
What does the xylem transport in plants?
What is the process of food transport through the phloem called?
In the ringing experiment, what is the first part of the plant to die when the bark with phloem is removed?
Which tropism causes the shoot to bend towards light?
What is the response of the root to gravity?
What did Charles Darwin conclude from his phototropism experiment on coleoptiles?
options: Light destroys growth hormones on the illuminated side
Auxin is produced in the roots and moves upward
The tip detects light and some influence is transmitted to regulate growth
Roots respond more strongly to light than shoots
questions.correctAnswer: The tip detects light and some influence is transmitted to regulate growth

questions.question: What is the characteristic feature of spongy mesophyll cells in a leaf?
options: They are columnar and tightly packed with many chloroplasts
They form a single layer of tightly packed cells on the leaf surface
They are irregularly shaped with numerous airspaces and fewer chloroplasts
They contain the largest number of chloroplasts per cell
questions.correctAnswer: They are irregularly shaped with numerous airspaces and fewer chloroplasts

questions.question: What shape are the guard cells that surround the stomata in the lower epidermis?
How are vascular bundles arranged in a typical dicot stem?
options: In one or two regular rings
Concentrated only in the pith region
questions.correctAnswer: In one or two regular rings

questions.question: What is the function of the pericycle in a dicot root?
options: It is the site of origin of lateral roots
It absorbs water and minerals from the soil
It stores starch grains as a food reserve
It transports food to different plant parts
questions.correctAnswer: It is the site of origin of lateral roots

questions.question: Cross-pollination refers to the transfer of pollen grains between:
options: Stamen and pistil of the same flower
Anther and filament within the same stamen

questions.question: What is the state of the endosperm in dicot seeds compared to monocot seeds?
options: Endosperm is large in dicot seeds and absent in monocot seeds
Endosperm is absent in dicot seeds and large in monocot seeds
Endosperm is present in both but larger in dicot seeds
Endosperm is absent in both types of seeds
questions.correctAnswer: Endosperm is absent in dicot seeds and large in monocot seeds

questions.question: Why is oxygen necessary for seed germination?
options: It is needed for aerobic respiration to provide energy for embryo growth
It helps hydrate the seed coat during germination
It activates the enzymes needed for photosynthesis
It provides carbon atoms for the growing seedling
questions.correctAnswer: It is needed for aerobic respiration to provide energy for embryo growth

questions.question: According to the cohesion-tension theory, what property of water helps hold the water column together within xylem vessels?
options: Adhesion of water to cell walls by hydrogen bonds
The high density of water molecules
Cohesion of water molecules by hydrogen bonds
questions.correctAnswer: Cohesion of water molecules by hydrogen bonds

questions.question: Which Ethiopian medicinal plant is traditionally used to treat tapeworm infection?
For what achievement was Professor Sebsebe Demissew awarded the Cuatrecases Medal in 2021?
options: For his lifetime work in conserving and recording the diverse Ethiopian flora
For being the first Sub-Saharan African elected to the Royal Society
For discovering new species of medicinal plants in Ethiopia
For establishing the Ethiopian National Herbarium in Addis Ababa
questions.correctAnswer: For his lifetime work in conserving and recording the diverse Ethiopian flora
